practiceExams.title: CLF-C02 Practice Exam 1: Foundation Check

domainCoverage.domain: AWS global infrastructure and core services
Billing, pricing, support, and cost controls

practiceExams.instructions: This is original Subul practice content for learning and readiness. It is not an official exam dump and does not reproduce protected exam items.

questions.question: A team is preparing for CLF-C02. A scenario emphasizes cloud concepts, value, and adoption and asks for a solution involving IAM. Which response is most exam-aligned?
options: Select IAM only after confirming requirements, identity boundaries, monitoring needs, and cost/reliability constraints.
Always select EC2 because it appears next to IAM in documentation.
Disable audit and monitoring to reduce setup effort during production rollout.
Use administrator-level permissions for every user and workload to avoid access errors.
questions.explanation: The best answer is requirement-driven. In the Cloud concepts, value, and adoption domain, the learner must map constraints to service behavior and include security, operations, reliability, and cost reasoning. The distractors ignore either requirements, governance, or safe operating practice.
whyOthersWrong: EC2 might be useful, but service adjacency is not a decision criterion.
Disabling observability hides failures and conflicts with production readiness.
Broad administrator access violates least privilege and creates avoidable risk.

options: Select Amazon Bedrock only after confirming requirements, identity boundaries, monitoring needs, and cost/reliability constraints.
Always select SageMaker because it appears next to Amazon Bedrock in documentation.
Disable audit and monitoring to reduce setup effort during production rollout.
Use administrator-level permissions for every user and workload to avoid access errors.
questions.explanation: The best answer is requirement-driven. In the AWS AI services and solution patterns domain, the learner must map constraints to service behavior and include security, operations, reliability, and cost reasoning. The distractors ignore either requirements, governance, or safe operating practice.
whyOthersWrong: SageMaker might be useful, but service adjacency is not a decision criterion.
Disabling observability hides failures and conflicts with production readiness.
Broad administrator access violates least privilege and creates avoidable risk.
referenceTopics: AWS AI services and solution patterns

Always select ConfigMaps because it appears next to Services in documentation.
Disable audit and monitoring to reduce setup effort during production rollout.
Use administrator-level permissions for every user and workload to avoid access errors.
questions.explanation: The best answer is requirement-driven. In the Container orchestration domain, the learner must map constraints to service behavior and include security, operations, reliability, and cost reasoning. The distractors ignore either requirements, governance, or safe operating practice.
whyOthersWrong: ConfigMaps might be useful, but service adjacency is not a decision criterion.
Disabling observability hides failures and conflicts with production readiness.
Broad administrator access violates least privilege and creates avoidable risk.

practiceExams.title: PL-300 Practice Exam 1: Data Preparation and Modeling
practiceExams.instructions: This is original Subul practice content for learning and readiness. It is not an official exam dump and does not reproduce protected exam items.

questions.question: A team preparing for PL-300 is working on Power Query transformations. The scenario focuses on Prepare the data. Which approach is most aligned with Microsoft best practices?
options: Start by mapping business and technical constraints, then choose services and controls accordingly.
Pick the most familiar service stack first and evaluate constraints later.
Defer security and monitoring decisions until after go-live.
Use broad administrator access and static capacity to reduce initial setup time.
questions.explanation: The best answer is requirement-driven and risk-aware. Certification scenarios usually reward a design that combines architecture fit, security, reliability, observability, and cost considerations.
whyOthersWrong: Familiarity is useful but does not replace requirement and risk analysis.
Postponing monitoring/security introduces operational and compliance gaps.
Over-privileged access and fixed overprovisioning create avoidable risk and waste.
